name: Marugame Udon
tagline: Locals genuinely love it despite the Waikiki address — watch them pull fresh noodles through the glass.
mustOrder: kamaage udon ($5–7, the purist's choice — plain dipping noodles, taste the quality)
parking: Don't drive — no parking in Waikiki
talkStory: Toridoll Holdings franchise from Kobe, Japan — modeled after Kagawa Prefecture self-serve udon shops.
insiderTips: Cafeteria-style: tray, order base udon, pick tempura, pay at register. Total bill can be under $10 — best value in Waikiki. Go at 10am opening or 2–4pm for shortest lines.
